cases.name: current-product-comparison
cases.prompt: Compare three current team note-taking products for a five-person research group.
must: states plan/tier, currency or region, and verification date
uses official pricing or product documentation for specifications
seeks independent evidence for usability or reliability
separates facts, inference, and recommendation
states what could change the recommendation
must_not: treats affiliate rankings as sole evidence
mixes free and paid tiers without saying so

cases.name: contested-scientific-claim
cases.prompt: Assess whether a scientific finding is established consensus.
must: defines the exact claim and population
uses primary studies plus synthesis or consensus evidence
records peer-review status and material limitations
describes credible disagreement rather than vote-counting papers
calibrates confidence
must_not: equates several agreeing papers with field-wide consensus

cases.name: policy-status
cases.prompt: Determine whether a policy is currently in force.
must: states jurisdiction
distinguishes enactment, publication, and effective dates
checks amendments, exceptions, and superseding authority
uses primary legal or official text
states informational-not-legal-advice boundary when appropriate
must_not: relies on an undated blog summary

cases.name: insufficient-evidence
cases.prompt: Research a niche claim for which no direct evidence is available.
must: answers supportable subparts
marks the central claim unverified
describes the missing evidence
does not invent citations
offers the safest next verification step
